name: Q3050137
abstract: Earl Bowman Swope (August 4, 1922 – January 3, 1968) was an American jazz trombonist.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: That was my first exposure to Earl Swope and he blew my head off, I’ve never played with a better trombone player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Washington, D.C., had ... Earl Swope on trombone ... These were all brilliant musicians. They had a small band together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, we had Earl Swope in that... Yeah, Earl Swope for awhile
